objective: Define governed local-repo and web/external research specialist lanes at governance source and define the safest downstream rollout model for pushing those org-level governance changes into consumer repos.
scope_boundary: Issue-backed plan, scope, handoff, review, and validation artifacts for the governance-source research-specialist and downstream-rollout contract work.

Governance personas, agents, packet/output schemas, standards/runbook text, validator surfaces, and deployer/verifier contract changes needed to add research specialists and define their downstream rollout path.
No direct product-repo mutation in this branch; downstream rollout remains a separate repo-specific step.
out_of_scope: Direct edits to Stampede, HealthcarePlatform, local-ai-machine, seek-and-ponder, knocktracker, ai-integration-services, or ASC-Evaluator in this branch.
Ad hoc web-search behavior that bypasses the governed packet/output contract.
Warning-only or documentation-only treatment of research specialist behavior.
Central GitHub setting mutation outside issue-backed governance apply work.
research_summary: Specialist research confirmed governance source already has packet-backed planner, auditor, QA, and sim-runner surfaces, but still lacks first-class governed specialists for local-repo discovery and web/external discovery. The same research confirmed the current downstream consumer package model is still a thin bootstrap path and needs an explicit package-managed versus repo-specific rollout model before these new specialist surfaces can be pushed safely into product repos.

sprints.name: Packet and review
sprints.goal: Capture the bounded governance-source research-specialist and downstream-rollout scope before implementation-ready promotion.
tasks: Write the issue-589 PDCAR, structured plan, planning execution scope, and planning handoff.
Record specialist research findings on local/web research lanes and the downstream rollout model.
Run the governed Claude review path against the packet before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: The issue-589 packet validates locally as planning_only.
The packet names both the governance-source specialist-lane gap and the downstream rollout-model gap.
Alternate-family review is recorded through the governed wrapper path before implementation-ready promotion.
If the governed wrapper fails closed, the packet records the failure evidence and blocking governance dependency, and the lane remains planning_only until the dependency is resolved.

sprints.name: Research specialist and rollout contract
sprints.goal: Land the minimum governance-source changes needed to add governed research specialists and define their downstream propagation model safely.
tasks: Add tracked local-repo and web/external research specialist personas and agent definitions backed by hldpro-sim.
Add packet/output contract surfaces so both research specialists accept only file-backed structured packets and return structured outputs.
Add machine-checkable source-attribution requirements for the web/external research lane.
Extend plan/handoff validation so research-lane usage and evidence refs can be proven structurally.
Update the governance package/deployer/verifier contract to distinguish package-managed consumer changes from repo-specific rollout PR work for these new specialist surfaces.
Add regression tests and local proof.
acceptance_criteria: A governed local-repo research specialist exists as a tracked, packet-backed, read-only governance lane.
A governed web/external research specialist exists as a tracked, packet-backed, read-only governance lane with hard-gated source attribution.
Plans and handoffs can declare which research specialist lane was used and validators can prove the packet/output artifact path.
The downstream rollout model is explicit about what is package-managed, what must remain repo-specific and issue-backed, and what remains report-only from the consumer side.
The strengthened specialist and rollout checks are covered by regression tests and local proof.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm what the current governance package can already deploy/verify into consumer repos and where the current limits are.
specialist_reviews.summary: The current deployer/verifier path remains a thin bootstrap model and does not yet perform true org-wide propagation. It can support safe downstream rollout only when the package manifest, desired-state contract, deployer, verifier, and rollback behavior are updated together.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm the governance constraints for adding local-repo and web/external research specialists and pushing those changes into consumer repos.
specialist_reviews.summary: Two research specialists fit the current SoM model only as bounded, read-only lanes under Codex orchestration. Web/external research should remain an exception lane with explicit source attribution. Downstream adoption must stay repo-specific and issue-backed even when package-managed consumer surfaces are extended.

alternate_model_review.reviewer: Claude specialist review via scripts/codex-review.sh claude
alternate_model_review.summary: Governed alternate-family review completed through scripts/codex-review.sh claude after refreshing this lane from merged origin/main. Verdict: accepted_with_followup. No blocking findings; duplicate next_execution_step metadata cleanup and final diff-hygiene rerun were the only follow-ups.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implementation-ready governance-source lane. Research-specialist and downstream rollout contract changes are now approved within this repo only; direct consumer-repo mutation remains out of scope.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the governed local-repo and web/external research specialist lanes, their packet/output contracts, and the downstream consumer rollout model surfaces defined in Sprint 2.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Issue-589 implementation stays bounded to governance-source surfaces only.
Direct product-repo changes stay out of scope for this branch.
The downstream rollout model must stay explicit about package-managed versus repo-specific issue-backed work.

material_deviation_rules: Do not implement the new research specialists by prose only; packet/output and validation surfaces must be part of the same slice.
Do not widen this branch into direct consumer-repo mutation work.
Do not treat consumer-side verification as equivalent to central GitHub setting application.
